workflows.title: Inline Code Edit Workflow
workflows.description: Make quick, targeted changes to specific code blocks
steps: Select code you want to change

workflows.title: Composer Multi-File Refactor
workflows.description: Make architectural changes across multiple files
steps: Describe task (e.g., 'Refactor auth to use JWT instead of sessions')
Review changes across all affected files
Accept all or selectively accept individual files

workflows.title: Chat with Apply Pattern
workflows.description: Explore solutions in chat, then apply code blocks
steps: Ask exploratory question (e.g., 'Show me how to add Redis caching')

workflows.title: Codebase Understanding
workflows.description: Learn about your codebase before making changes
steps: Use @codebase in chat for broad questions
Ask about patterns, architecture, or specific functions
Build mental model before making edits

proTips: Use @codebase for broad questions about architecture and patterns
Use @file and @folder to narrow context and improve accuracy
Don't overload chat with too many files—focus context is key
Select exact code you want to change before Cmd+K
Be specific in instructions: 'Add error handling to the API fetch' is better than 'Fix the code'
Use Composer for multi-file changes, not Chat with Apply
Clear chat with Cmd+Shift+L when switching to a different task
Review diffs carefully before accepting, especially in multi-file edits
Test changes immediately after applying to catch issues
Use .cursorrules file in project root to enforce consistent AI behavior
Enable 'Auto-Apply Edits' only for simple, low-risk changes
Model selection matters: cursor-small is fast/free, claude-opus is best quality
